ollama_format.description: Structured smoke output schema for one text response.
ollama_format.title: StructuredSmokeText

structured_output_schema.description: Structured smoke output schema for one text response.
structured_output_schema.title: StructuredSmokeText

parsed_response.response_text: The data reveals a gradual increase in the number of households engaged in social learning (household-SL?) across timesteps, with values remaining consistently zero until timestep 19. At this point, counts begin to rise slowly but steadily, reaching a peak of 48 households by timestep 29. The trend shows intermittent fluctuations but no significant decline after peaking. This suggests that even within a homogeneous population of E-RO agents (with social-susceptibility initially set to 0), a subset of households initiated social learning, potentially linked to climate variability-driven resource pressures or endogenous learning mechanisms. By timestep 29, SL adoption stabilized around 14–48 households, indicating heightened social learning adoption in later stages. These dynamics imply that under homogeneous conditions, behavioral flexibility within E-RO agents may allow limited adoption of social learning traits, which could enhance resilience to climatic shocks by enabling information sharing. For decision-makers, this underscores the potential value of fostering social learning mechanisms even in systems dominated by baseline behavioral strategies, particularly in variable environments.
